meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Deployment
content.seo_description: Overcome deployment roadblocks with our Antigravity project template. A high-impact, intermediate guide for students building their first AI-powered SaaS apps.
content.answer_block: The 'can't deploy' bottleneck usually stems from environment configuration drift. By utilizing the Antigravity project template, you standardize your infrastructure-as-code, ensuring your local environment mirrors production. Paired with Claude Code, you can automate containerization and cloud provisioning, turning your prototype into a globally accessible, production-ready application in minutes.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks perfecting your logic, tuning your prompts, and crafting a beautiful UI. Yet, when it comes time to push to production, the dream hits a wall. The terminal spits out cryptic errors, dependencies clash, and your app remains trapped on your local machine—a ghost of what could be a career-defining project.

This is the rite of passage for every developer. The gap between 'working on my machine' and 'shipped to the world' is bridged not by luck, but by architecture. With the Antigravity template and the power of Claude Code, you will stop fighting deployment and start scaling your vision.

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity Template
steps.description: Scaffold your project using the Antigravity base to ensure your environment is pre-configured for modern deployment standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the standard Antigravity template with support for Next.js and Supabase integration.

steps.title: Configure Infrastructure-as-Code
steps.description: Define your cloud resources programmatically so your deployment environment is consistent every time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Terraform configuration for an AWS ECS deployment with a standard PostgreSQL database instance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate terraform files for ecs and rds'

steps.title: Automate Dockerization
steps.description: Containerize your app to eliminate the 'it works on my machine' syndrome once and for all.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a multi-stage Dockerfile optimized for a Node.js production build, minimizing image size.

steps.title: Implement CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Set up automated tests and deployment triggers so every push to main is a deploy to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, tests, and deploys to AWS on push to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'setup github actions pipeline for deployment'

steps.title: Final Environment Validation
steps.description: Perform a dry-run deployment to ensure all environment variables and secrets are correctly mapped.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current configuration and suggest a checklist for a production-ready environment variable setup.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'check environment variables and validate deployment config'

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file to track required secrets without exposing your production keys to Git.
Containerize your database migrations to ensure your schema updates automatically during the deployment phase.
Use ephemeral preview environments for every pull request to test features in isolation before merging to main.
Monitor your deployment logs with a centralized service like Sentry to catch runtime errors before your users do.
Treat your infrastructure as code; if you change a setting in the dashboard, you have already failed the audit.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, focus on setting up telemetry and user analytics. Use the feedback loop to iterate on your features, treating your first deployment as the foundation for your next version.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint to stop struggling with deployment and start building your legacy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
